title: Researchers advance tech that could help scientists detect habitable worlds beyond our solar system
excerpt: Are we alone in the universe? For scientists working on NASA's proposed Habitable Worlds Observatory, that question is no longer purely philosophical. It is increasingly becoming an engineering problem.
content_html:
Are we alone in the universe? For scientists working on NASA's proposed Habitable Worlds Observatory, that question is no longer purely philosophical. It is increasingly becoming an engineering problem.

Researchers at UCF's College of Optics and Photonics (CREOL) are helping develop technology designed to help future space telescopes detect potentially habitable planets orbiting distant stars.

The project, known as PEEPSS (Photonics-Enabled Exoplanet Spectroscopic System), aims to help astronomers directly observe planets hidden within the overwhelming brightness of their parent stars.

"If they're in the habitable zone, that means they are orbiting close to their host star, and that host star is typically going to be 10 billion times brighter than the planet," says Stephen Eikenberry, a professor and principal investigator on the project.

To explain the difficulty, Eikenberry compares the task to trying to spot "a tiny blinking light while someone is shining a spotlight directly in your face."

The work supports the long-term goals of NASA's proposed Habitable Worlds Observatory (HWO), a future flagship space telescope intended to search for Earth-like planets beyond our solar system and analyze their atmospheres for signs of life.

Solving one of astronomy's hardest problems

Astronomers already know planets are common throughout the universe. The challenge now is identifying Earth-like planets that are extraordinarily faint compared with the stars they orbit.

Astronomers use instruments called coronagraphs to block a star's glare while allowing faint planetary signals to reach a telescope's detectors.

Even then, microscopic imperfections in a telescope's optics can allow enormous amounts of starlight to leak through the system.

"And you can say, 'Well, that's only one part in a million,'" Eikenberry says. "Guess what? A part in a million means it's still 10,000 times brighter than your exoplanet. You're doomed."

The system performs an advanced form of wavefront sensing that detects and corrects tiny distortions in incoming light before they overwhelm planetary signals.

Unlike many existing systems that monitor light earlier in the optical process, PEEPSS performs wavefront sensing directly at the telescope's focal plane, the same location where scientific imaging occurs.

That distinction is important because it allows researchers to detect and correct optical errors that emerge after light passes through a telescope's coronagraph. Scientists refer to these distortions as "non-common-path aberrations."

To explain the concept, Eikenberry compares the system to trying to monitor a room you cannot fully see.

"Imagine you're in a house and you want the entire house to be perfectly clean," he says. "You can see people walking into the bedroom, but you can't actually see inside the bedroom itself. That's the non-common path."

By monitoring the complete optical pathway to the focal plane, researchers hope PEEPSS can help future observatories achieve the extraordinary precision necessary to detect habitable worlds.

A new approach using photonic lanterns

At the center of the project is an emerging technology known as a photonic lantern.

The device separates complex incoming light into individual optical channels, allowing researchers to recover not only brightness information but also phase information carried by light waves, data that conventional imaging systems typically discard.

"Traditional detectors wipe that information out," Eikenberry says. "Photonic lanterns allow us to recover it."

That additional information enables what researchers describe as "quantum-inspired imaging," an emerging technique that uses light behavior to improve image resolution and filter out remaining starlight.

Researchers at CREOL have become major contributors to the rapidly growing field of astrophotonics, which combines astronomy, fiber optics and advanced photonic technologies.

"There are really only two major centers doing cutting-edge work on photonic lanterns," Eikenberry says. "Us and the University of Sydney in Australia."

The project brings together collaborators from UCF, the University of California, Santa Cruz, the University of Sydney and the Space Telescope Science Institute. At UCF, Eikenberry works alongside graduate student Genevieve Markees and researchers including Rodrigo Amezcua Correa, Miguel Bandres and Jose-Enrique Antonio-Lopez, whose expertise in fiber optics and photonics helped establish the collaboration.

Looking toward habitable worlds

The current PEEPSS project is structured as a three-year effort focused on building and testing prototype photonic lantern systems in laboratory and telescope environments.

Some versions of the technology have already undergone testing on telescopes in Hawaii through collaborations with the Air Force Research Laboratory and international research partners.

Ultimately, researchers hope the technology could become part of future NASA missions searching for habitable planets around distant stars.

"If we can identify habitable worlds around other stars and show they possess conditions where Earth-like life could survive, that's already revolutionary," Eikenberry says. "If we discover actual evidence of life, then we're talking about one of the greatest scientific discoveries in human history."

For Eikenberry, humanity may now be approaching a historic turning point.

"We are one mission away," he says.

And if future observations succeed, humanity may no longer simply wonder whether life exists elsewhere in the universe. For researchers involved in the project, that possibility is what makes the work so compelling.

"We'll look up and know."

Who's behind this story?

Sadie Harley

BSc Life Sciences & Ecology. Microbiology lab background with pharmaceutical news experience in oil, gas, and renewable industries. Full profile →

Andrew Zinin

Master's in physics with research experience. Long-time science news enthusiast. Plays key role in Science X's editorial success. Full profile →

sentences.en: UCF graduate students Liza Fernanda Quinn Reyes (foreground) and Genevieve Markees work with photonic lantern fabrication equipment in a CREOL laboratory.
sentences.zh: UCF 研究生 Liza Fernanda Quinn Reyes（前景）和 Genevieve Markees 在 CREOL 实验室中使用光子灯笼制造设备。

sentences.en: The technology is being developed as part of the PEEPSS project to improve future exoplanet imaging.
sentences.zh: 该技术是 PEEPSS 项目的一部分，旨在改善未来的系外行星成像。

sentences.en: Credit: Antoine Hart / University of Central Florida
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Antoine Hart / 中佛罗里达大学

blocks.html_en: Are we alone in the universe? For scientists working on NASA's proposed Habitable Worlds Observatory, that question is no longer purely philosophical. It is increasingly becoming an engineering problem.

sentences.en: Are we alone in the universe?
sentences.zh: 我们在宇宙中是孤独的吗？

sentences.en: For scientists working on NASA's proposed Habitable Worlds Observatory, that question is no longer purely philosophical.
sentences.zh: 对于在美国宇航局提议的宜居世界天文台工作的科学家来说，这个问题不再纯粹是哲学问题。

sentences.en: It is increasingly becoming an engineering problem.
sentences.zh: 它日益成为一个工程问题。

blocks.html_en: Researchers at UCF's College of Optics and Photonics (CREOL) are helping develop technology designed to help future space telescopes detect potentially habitable planets orbiting distant stars.

sentences.en: Researchers at UCF's College of Optics and Photonics (CREOL) are helping develop technology designed to help future space telescopes detect potentially habitable planets orbiting distant stars.
sentences.zh: UCF 光学与光子学院 (CREOL) 的研究人员正在帮助开发技术，旨在帮助未来的太空望远镜探测绕遥远恒星运行的潜在宜居行星。

blocks.html_en: The project, known as PEEPSS (Photonics-Enabled Exoplanet Spectroscopic System), aims to help astronomers directly observe planets hidden within the overwhelming brightness of their parent stars.

sentences.en: The project, known as PEEPSS (Photonics-Enabled Exoplanet Spectroscopic System), aims to help astronomers directly observe planets hidden within the overwhelming brightness of their parent stars.
sentences.zh: 该项目被称为 PEEPSS（光子学系外行星光谱系统），旨在帮助天文学家直接观察隐藏在母恒星压倒性亮度中的行星。

blocks.html_en: "If they're in the habitable zone, that means they are orbiting close to their host star, and that host star is typically going to be 10 billion times brighter than the planet," says Stephen Eikenberry, a professor and principal investigator on the project.

sentences.en: "If they're in the habitable zone, that means they are orbiting close to their host star, and that host star is typically going to be 10 billion times brighter than the planet," says Stephen Eikenberry, a professor and principal investigator on the project.
sentences.zh: “如果它们位于宜居带，那就意味着它们的轨道靠近主星，而主星通常比行星亮 100 亿倍，”该项目的教授兼首席研究员斯蒂芬·艾肯伯里 (Stephen Eikenberry) 说道。

blocks.html_en: To explain the difficulty, Eikenberry compares the task to trying to spot "a tiny blinking light while someone is shining a spotlight directly in your face."

sentences.en: To explain the difficulty, Eikenberry compares the task to trying to spot "a tiny blinking light while someone is shining a spotlight directly in your face."
sentences.zh: 为了解释这一困难，艾肯伯里将这项任务比作“当有人将聚光灯直接照射在你的脸上时，试图发现微小的闪烁光”。

blocks.html_en: The work supports the long-term goals of NASA's proposed Habitable Worlds Observatory (HWO), a future flagship space telescope intended to search for Earth-like planets beyond our solar system and analyze their atmospheres for signs of life.

sentences.en: The work supports the long-term goals of NASA's proposed Habitable Worlds Observatory (HWO), a future flagship space telescope intended to search for Earth-like planets beyond our solar system and analyze their atmospheres for signs of life.
sentences.zh: 这项工作支持美国宇航局提议的宜居世界天文台（HWO）的长期目标，这是一个未来的旗舰太空望远镜，旨在寻找太阳系之外的类地行星，并分析其大气层是否存在生命迹象。

sentences.zh: UCF 研究人员使用精密制造设备为 PEEPSS 项目开发光子灯。

sentences.en: The technology is designed to improve future observations of Earth-like exoplanets.
sentences.zh: 该技术旨在改善未来对类地系外行星的观测。

sentences.en: Credit: Antoine Hart / University of Central Florida
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Antoine Hart / 中佛罗里达大学

blocks.html_en: Solving one of astronomy's hardest problems

sentences.zh: 解决天文学最困难的问题之一

blocks.html_en: Astronomers already know planets are common throughout the universe. The challenge now is identifying Earth-like planets that are extraordinarily faint compared with the stars they orbit.

sentences.en: Astronomers already know planets are common throughout the universe.
sentences.zh: 天文学家已经知道行星在整个宇宙中很常见。

sentences.en: The challenge now is identifying Earth-like planets that are extraordinarily faint compared with the stars they orbit.
sentences.zh: 现在的挑战是识别与它们绕轨道运行的恒星相比极其微弱的类地行星。

blocks.html_en: Astronomers use instruments called coronagraphs to block a star's glare while allowing faint planetary signals to reach a telescope's detectors.

sentences.en: Astronomers use instruments called coronagraphs to block a star's glare while allowing faint planetary signals to reach a telescope's detectors.
sentences.zh: 天文学家使用日冕仪来阻挡恒星的眩光，同时允许微弱的行星信号到达望远镜的探测器。

blocks.html_en: Even then, microscopic imperfections in a telescope's optics can allow enormous amounts of starlight to leak through the system.

sentences.en: Even then, microscopic imperfections in a telescope's optics can allow enormous amounts of starlight to leak through the system.
sentences.zh: 即便如此，望远镜光学器件的微观缺陷也可能导致大量星光通过系统泄漏。

blocks.html_en: "And you can say, 'Well, that's only one part in a million,'" Eikenberry says. "Guess what? A part in a million means it's still 10,000 times brighter than your exoplanet. You're doomed."

sentences.en: "And you can say, 'Well, that's only one part in a million,'" Eikenberry says.
sentences.zh: “你可以说，‘好吧，这只是百万分之一，’”艾肯伯里说。

sentences.en: "Guess what?
sentences.zh: “你猜怎么着？

sentences.en: A part in a million means it's still 10,000 times brighter than your exoplanet.
sentences.zh: 百万分之一意味着它仍然比系外行星亮一万倍。

sentences.en: You're doomed."
sentences.zh: 你注定要失败。”

blocks.html_en: The system performs an advanced form of wavefront sensing that detects and corrects tiny distortions in incoming light before they overwhelm planetary signals.

sentences.en: The system performs an advanced form of wavefront sensing that detects and corrects tiny distortions in incoming light before they overwhelm planetary signals.
sentences.zh: 该系统执行一种先进的波前传感技术，可以在入射光淹没行星信号之前检测并纠正它们的微小畸变。

blocks.html_en: Unlike many existing systems that monitor light earlier in the optical process, PEEPSS performs wavefront sensing directly at the telescope's focal plane, the same location where scientific imaging occurs.

sentences.en: Unlike many existing systems that monitor light earlier in the optical process, PEEPSS performs wavefront sensing directly at the telescope's focal plane, the same location where scientific imaging occurs.
sentences.zh: 与许多在光学过程早期监测光的现有系统不同，PEEPSS 直接在望远镜的焦平面（科学成像发生的位置）进行波前传感。

blocks.html_en: That distinction is important because it allows researchers to detect and correct optical errors that emerge after light passes through a telescope's coronagraph. Scientists refer to these distortions as "non-common-path aberrations."

sentences.en: That distinction is important because it allows researchers to detect and correct optical errors that emerge after light passes through a telescope's coronagraph.
sentences.zh: 这种区别很重要，因为它使研究人员能够检测并纠正光线通过望远镜日冕仪后出现的光学误差。

sentences.en: Scientists refer to these distortions as "non-common-path aberrations."
sentences.zh: 科学家将这些畸变称为“非共路径像差”。

blocks.html_en: To explain the concept, Eikenberry compares the system to trying to monitor a room you cannot fully see.

sentences.en: To explain the concept, Eikenberry compares the system to trying to monitor a room you cannot fully see.
sentences.zh: 为了解释这个概念，Eikenberry 将系统比作试图监控一个你无法完全看到的房间。

blocks.html_en: "Imagine you're in a house and you want the entire house to be perfectly clean," he says. "You can see people walking into the bedroom, but you can't actually see inside the bedroom itself. That's the non-common path."

sentences.en: "Imagine you're in a house and you want the entire house to be perfectly clean," he says.
sentences.zh: “想象一下你在一所房子里，你希望整个房子都非常干净，”他说。

sentences.en: "You can see people walking into the bedroom, but you can't actually see inside the bedroom itself.
sentences.zh: “你可以看到人们走进卧室，但你实际上看不到卧室本身。

sentences.en: That's the non-common path."
sentences.zh: 这是不寻常的道路。”

blocks.html_en: By monitoring the complete optical pathway to the focal plane, researchers hope PEEPSS can help future observatories achieve the extraordinary precision necessary to detect habitable worlds.

sentences.en: By monitoring the complete optical pathway to the focal plane, researchers hope PEEPSS can help future observatories achieve the extraordinary precision necessary to detect habitable worlds.
sentences.zh: 通过监测到焦平面的完整光学路径，研究人员希望 PEEPSS 能够帮助未来的天文台实现探测宜居世界所需的非凡精度。

blocks.html_en: "If we can identify habitable worlds around other stars and show they possess conditions where Earth-like life could survive, that's already revolutionary," Eikenberry says. "If we discover actual evidence of life, then we're talking about one of the greatest scientific discoveries in human history."

sentences.en: "If we can identify habitable worlds around other stars and show they possess conditions where Earth-like life could survive, that's already revolutionary," Eikenberry says.
sentences.zh: 艾肯伯里说：“如果我们能够识别其他恒星周围的宜居世界，并证明它们具备类地生命可以生存的条件，那已经是革命性的了。”

sentences.en: "If we discover actual evidence of life, then we're talking about one of the greatest scientific discoveries in human history."
sentences.zh: “如果我们发现了生命的实际证据，那么我们正在谈论人类历史上最伟大的科学发现之一。”

blocks.html_en: For Eikenberry, humanity may now be approaching a historic turning point.

sentences.en: For Eikenberry, humanity may now be approaching a historic turning point.
sentences.zh: 对于艾肯伯里来说，人类现在可能正在接近一个历史性的转折点。

blocks.html_en: "We are one mission away," he says.

sentences.en: "We are one mission away," he says.
sentences.zh: “我们还差一个任务，”他说。

blocks.html_en: And if future observations succeed, humanity may no longer simply wonder whether life exists elsewhere in the universe. For researchers involved in the project, that possibility is what makes the work so compelling.

sentences.en: And if future observations succeed, humanity may no longer simply wonder whether life exists elsewhere in the universe.
sentences.zh: 如果未来的观测成功，人类可能不再只是想知道宇宙其他地方是否存在生命。

sentences.en: For researchers involved in the project, that possibility is what makes the work so compelling.
sentences.zh: 对于参与该项目的研究人员来说，这种可能性使得这项工作如此引人注目。

blocks.html_en: "We'll look up and know."

sentences.en: "We'll look up and know."
sentences.zh: “我们抬头看看就知道了。”

sentences.en: Who's behind this story?

content_html_zh:
我们在宇宙中是孤独的吗？ 对于在美国宇航局提议的宜居世界天文台工作的科学家来说，这个问题不再纯粹是哲学问题。 它日益成为一个工程问题。

UCF 光学与光子学院 (CREOL) 的研究人员正在帮助开发技术，旨在帮助未来的太空望远镜探测绕遥远恒星运行的潜在宜居行星。

该项目被称为 PEEPSS（光子学系外行星光谱系统），旨在帮助天文学家直接观察隐藏在母恒星压倒性亮度中的行星。

“如果它们位于宜居带，那就意味着它们的轨道靠近主星，而主星通常比行星亮 100 亿倍，”该项目的教授兼首席研究员斯蒂芬·艾肯伯里 (Stephen Eikenberry) 说道。

为了解释这一困难，艾肯伯里将这项任务比作“当有人将聚光灯直接照射在你的脸上时，试图发现微小的闪烁光”。

这项工作支持美国宇航局提议的宜居世界天文台（HWO）的长期目标，这是一个未来的旗舰太空望远镜，旨在寻找太阳系之外的类地行星，并分析其大气层是否存在生命迹象。

解决天文学最困难的问题之一

天文学家已经知道行星在整个宇宙中很常见。 现在的挑战是识别与它们绕轨道运行的恒星相比极其微弱的类地行星。

天文学家使用日冕仪来阻挡恒星的眩光，同时允许微弱的行星信号到达望远镜的探测器。

即便如此，望远镜光学器件的微观缺陷也可能导致大量星光通过系统泄漏。

“你可以说，‘好吧，这只是百万分之一，’”艾肯伯里说。 “你猜怎么着？ 百万分之一意味着它仍然比系外行星亮一万倍。 你注定要失败。”

该系统执行一种先进的波前传感技术，可以在入射光淹没行星信号之前检测并纠正它们的微小畸变。

与许多在光学过程早期监测光的现有系统不同，PEEPSS 直接在望远镜的焦平面（科学成像发生的位置）进行波前传感。

这种区别很重要，因为它使研究人员能够检测并纠正光线通过望远镜日冕仪后出现的光学误差。 科学家将这些畸变称为“非共路径像差”。

为了解释这个概念，Eikenberry 将系统比作试图监控一个你无法完全看到的房间。

“想象一下你在一所房子里，你希望整个房子都非常干净，”他说。 “你可以看到人们走进卧室，但你实际上看不到卧室本身。 这是不寻常的道路。”

通过监测到焦平面的完整光学路径，研究人员希望 PEEPSS 能够帮助未来的天文台实现探测宜居世界所需的非凡精度。

使用光子灯笼的新方法

该项目的核心是一种被称为光子灯笼的新兴技术。

该设备将复杂的入射光分成单独的光学通道，使研究人员不仅可以恢复亮度信息，还可以恢复光波携带的相位信息，而传统成像系统通常会丢弃这些数据。

“传统探测器会消除这些信息，”艾肯伯里说。 “光子灯笼让我们能够恢复它。”

这些附加信息使研究人员能够实现“量子启发成像”，这是一种利用光行为来提高图像分辨率并滤除剩余星光的新兴技术。

CREOL 的研究人员已成为快速发展的天文学领域的主要贡献者，该领域结合了天文学、光纤和先进的光子技术。

“实际上只有两个主要中心在光子灯笼方面开展前沿工作，”艾肯伯里说。 “我们和澳大利亚悉尼大学。”

该项目汇集了来自中佛罗里达大学、加州大学圣克鲁斯分校、悉尼大学和太空望远镜科学研究所的合作者。 在中佛罗里达大学，Eikenberry 与研究生 Genevieve Markees 以及 Rodrigo Amezcua Correa、Miguel Bandres 和 Jose-Enrique Antonio-Lopez 等研究人员一起工作，他们在光纤和光子学方面的专业知识帮助建立了合作。

展望宜居世界

目前的 PEEPSS 项目为期三年，重点是在实验室和望远镜环境中构建和测试原型光子灯笼系统。

通过与空军研究实验室和国际研究伙伴的合作，该技术的某些版本已经在夏威夷的望远镜上进行了测试。

最终，研究人员希望这项技术能够成为美国宇航局未来寻找遥远恒星周围宜居行星任务的一部分。

艾肯伯里说：“如果我们能够识别其他恒星周围的宜居世界，并证明它们具备类地生命可以生存的条件，那已经是革命性的了。” “如果我们发现了生命的实际证据，那么我们正在谈论人类历史上最伟大的科学发现之一。”

对于艾肯伯里来说，人类现在可能正在接近一个历史性的转折点。

“我们还差一个任务，”他说。

如果未来的观测成功，人类可能不再只是想知道宇宙其他地方是否存在生命。 对于参与该项目的研究人员来说，这种可能性使得这项工作如此引人注目。

“我们抬头看看就知道了。”

谁是这个故事的幕后黑手？

萨迪·哈雷

生命科学与生态学理学士。 微生物学实验室背景，具有石油、天然气和可再生行业的制药新闻经验。 完整档案 →

安德鲁·齐宁
